0.b1: This is the foundation of creative thinking.
0.cn: 这是创造性思维的基础。

1.original: Here are suggestions to help you develop creative power through belief.
1.b1: Here are some tips to help you build creative power through belief.
1.cn: 这里有一些建议，可以帮助你通过信念来培养创造力。

3.original: Eliminate the word impossible from your thinking and speaking vocabularies.
3.b1: Stop using the word 'impossible' when you think or speak.
3.cn: 在你的思考和言谈中，删掉“不可能”这个词。

4.cn: “不可能”是一个导致失败的词。

5.original: The thought it's impossible sets off a chain reaction of other thoughts to prove you're right.
5.b1: Thinking something is impossible makes you look for reasons to prove you are right.
5.cn: 认为某事不可能，会引发一系列想法来证明你是对的。

7.original: Think of something you've been wanting to do but felt you couldn't.
7.b1: Think about something you wanted to do but felt you could not.
7.cn: 想想一件你想做却觉得自己做不到的事情。

8.original: Now make a list of reasons why you can do it.
8.b1: Now, make a list of reasons why you can do it.
8.cn: 现在，列出你能做到它的理由。

9.original: Many of us whip and defeat our desires simply because we concentrate on why we can't,
9.b1: Many of us fail because we only focus on why we cannot do things.
9.cn: 我们许多人之所以失败，仅仅是因为我们只关注为什么我们做不到。

10.original: when the only thing worthy of our mental concentration is why we can.
10.b1: Instead, we should focus on why we can.
10.cn: 而我们真正应该关注的，是我们为什么能做到。

11.original: Recently I read a newspaper item that said there are too many counties in most states.
11.b1: I recently read an article saying most states have too many counties.
11.cn: 最近我读到一篇报纸文章，说大多数州里的县太多了。

12.original: The article pointed out that most county boundaries were established decades before the first automobile was built,
12.b1: The article said these borders were created before cars existed.
12.cn: 文章指出，大多数县的边界是在第一辆汽车制造出来几十年前确定的。

13.original: and while the horse and buggy was the chief mode of travel.
13.b1: Back then, people mostly traveled by horse and carriage.
13.cn: 当时，马车是主要的交通方式。

14.original: But today, with fast automobiles and good roads, there is no reason why three or four counties could not be combined.
14.b1: Today, with fast cars and good roads, we could easily combine several counties.
14.cn: 但今天，有了快速的汽车和良好的道路，没有理由不能将三四个县合并在一起。

15.original: This would cut down greatly on duplicated services so that taxpayers would actually get better service for less money.
15.b1: This would reduce waste and give taxpayers better service for less money.
15.cn: 这会大大减少重复的服务，让纳税人花更少的钱得到更好的服务。

16.original: The writer of this article said he thought he had stumbled across a really live idea,
16.b1: The writer thought he had a great new idea.
16.cn: 这篇文章的作者认为他发现了一个非常棒的好点子。

17.original: so he interviewed 30 people at random to get their reactions.
17.b1: So, he asked 30 people what they thought about it.
17.cn: 于是他随机采访了30个人，想听听他们的反应。

19.original: Not one person thought the idea had merit, even though it would provide them with better local government at less cost.
19.b1: No one liked the idea, even though it would improve government and save money.
19.cn: 没有一个人认为这个想法有价值，尽管它能以更低的成本提供更好的地方政府服务。

20.original: That's an example of traditional thinking.
20.b1: That is an example of traditional thinking.
20.cn: 这就是传统思维的一个例子。

21.original: The traditional thinker's mind is paralyzed.
21.b1: The mind of a traditional thinker is stuck.
21.cn: 传统思维者的思想是僵化的。

22.original: He reasons, it's been this way for a hundred years, therefore it must be good and must stay this way.
22.b1: They think that because things have been the same for a hundred years, they must stay that way.
22.cn: 他认为，既然一百年来都是这样，那它一定很好，而且必须保持这样。

23.b1: Why take the risk of changing?
23.cn: 为什么要冒改变的风险呢？

24.original: Average people have always resented progress.
24.b1: Average people have always disliked progress.

25.original: Many voiced a protest toward the automobile on the grounds that nature meant for us to walk or use horses.
25.b1: Many people protested against cars, saying we should only walk or use horses.
25.cn: 许多人曾抗议汽车，理由是自然注定我们要走路或骑马。

26.original: The airplane seemed drastic to many.
26.b1: The airplane seemed too extreme to many people.
26.cn: 对许多人来说，飞机看起来太激进了。

27.original: Man had no right to enter the province reserved for birds.
27.b1: They thought humans had no right to fly like birds.
27.cn: 他们认为人类没有权利进入属于鸟类的领域。

28.original: A lot of status-quoers still insist that man has no business in space.
28.b1: Many people still insist that humans do not belong in space.
28.cn: 许多墨守成规的人仍然坚持认为人类不该去太空。

29.original: One top missile expert recently gave an answer to this kind of thinking.
29.b1: A top missile expert recently answered this type of thinking.
29.cn: 一位顶尖的导弹专家最近对这种想法做出了回应。

30.original: Man belongs, says Dr. Von Braun, where man wants to go.
30.b1: Dr. Von Braun says humans belong wherever they want to go.
30.cn: 冯·布劳恩博士说，人类想去哪里，就属于哪里。

31.original: Around 1900, a sales executive discovered a scientific principle of sales management.
31.b1: Around 1900, a sales manager found a scientific way to manage sales.
31.cn: 1900年左右，一位销售主管发现了一条销售管理的科学原则。

32.original: It received a lot of publicity and even found its way into textbooks.
32.b1: It became very famous and was even put into textbooks.
32.cn: 它获得了广泛的宣传，甚至被写进了教科书。

34.original: There is one best way to sell a product.
34.b1: There is only one best way to sell a product.
34.cn: 销售产品有一种最好的方法。

35.original: Find the best way, then never deviate from it.
35.b1: Find that way and never change it.
35.cn: 找到那个方法，然后永远不要偏离它。

36.original: Fortunately for this man's company, new leadership came in in time to save the organization from financial ruin.
36.b1: Luckily, new leaders arrived just in time to save the company from going broke.
36.cn: 幸运的是，新领导层及时上任，挽救了这家公司，使其免于破产。

37.original: Contrast that experience with the philosophy of Crawford H. Greenwalt, president of one of the nation's largest business organizations,
37.b1: Compare that to the ideas of Crawford H. Greenwalt, the head of a huge company.
37.cn: 将那种经历与克劳福德·H·格林沃尔特的理念进行对比，他是全国最大的商业组织之一的总裁。

38.b1: The company is E. I. DuPont de Nemours.

39.original: In a talk at Columbia University, Mr. Greenwalt said, There are many ways in which a good job can be done,
39.b1: In a speech, Mr. Greenwalt said there are many ways to do a good job.
39.cn: 在一次演讲中，格林沃尔特先生说，做好工作有很多种方法。

40.original: as many ways, in fact, as there are men to whom the task is given.
40.b1: In fact, there are as many ways as there are people doing the job.
40.cn: 事实上，有多少人去做这项工作，就有多少种方法。

41.original: In truth, there is no one best way to do anything.
41.b1: Truly, there is no single best way to do anything.
41.cn: 事实上，做任何事情都没有唯一最好的方法。

42.original: There is no one best way to decorate an apartment, landscape a lawn,
42.b1: There is no best way to decorate a room or landscape a garden.
42.cn: 装饰公寓或美化草坪没有最好的方法。

43.original: make a sail, rear a child, or cook a steak.
43.b1: There is no best way to make a sail, raise a child, or cook a steak.
43.cn: 制作帆、抚养孩子或烹饪牛排也没有最好的方法。

44.original: There are as many best ways as there are creative minds.
44.b1: There are as many best ways as there are creative minds.
44.cn: 有多少创造性的头脑，就有多少种最好的方法。

46.original: If we let tradition freeze our minds, new ideas can't sprout.
46.b1: If we let tradition freeze our minds, we cannot have new ideas.
46.cn: 如果我们让传统冻结了我们的思想，新想法就无法萌芽。
